lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. What are you doing all the way out here?

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Just... looking around.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, did you lose something?

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: No... I found something. A door.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A door? I see doors all the time. What makes this one so special?

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: It's buried beneath a tonne of rocks. Could be the entrance to some ancient ruins...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow! Ancient ruins? Are you an archaeologist or something?

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Archaeologist? Me? Hmm...

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Of course I am! I'm the greatest archaeologist of our time. The name's Willow! Have you not heard of me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid I haven't. Are you looking for a way into the ruins?

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: You bet I am, but I don't think there's any other way in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh dear. Do you have another plan to get in?

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Potentially, but I won't be able to do it on my own. Nor can I leave the area. If I'm not here to guard the entrance, some other archaeologist might come along and steal the glory from me!

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Maybe you could give me a hand? There could be untold riches inside the ruins!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A chance to uncover ancient ruins doesn't happen every day! Of course I'll help! I'm [player name] by the way. How do you plan to get in?

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: The entrance is covered in rocks and rubble - I don't think mining them will work.

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: We'll need something stronger. Who knows what could be inside the ruins. There could be traps, or even monsters!

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: No, I think we'll need to get my old crew...

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: ...I mean, excavation team together. But you'll need to convince them to help us. We didn't part on the best of terms.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened?

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: We had a falling out after a, uhm... dig, went wrong.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find your old team, and how will they help?

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: To get into the ruins we'll need to blow up all the rocks blocking the entrance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That doesn't sound too safe. What if we damage the ruins? Surely it's better to go slower and preserve as much as we can?

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: No! We need to get in there as soon as possible! Burntof the dwarf will do it. He's a master with explosives.

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: From what I can remember, he's a fan of his drink. His favourite pub being the Rising Sun Inn, in Falador - I'd look there first.

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: After we've blown the rocks away, we'll need someone to move all the rubble. Someone strong.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I could do it! I'm quite strong myself.

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Ha ha ha! I don't think so. We need a real [strongman/strongwoman] - someone with muscles. An old friend of mine, Checkal, should be able to do all the heavy lifting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh okay... Where can I find him?

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Hmm... We had quite a rough falling out when we last spoke, but I heard he's hanging around Barbarian Village.

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Lastly, if my hunch is correct, this place will be covered in traps. We'll need my pal Marley to disarm them. He's the best thief I know. Been doing it for years.

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: I have no idea where he is these days. But his cousin Charlie in Varrock may know. You should talk to him. He's normally somewhere near the Blue Moon Inn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You have a rather interesting group of friends... but if they get us into the ruins, I'm all for it.

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Good luck. Once you've recruited one of the team, I'll meet them at the entrance. It's on the west side of the mountain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Seems like you're in a bit of a pickle. Have fun with that.

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Back again? What can I help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find Marley?

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Marley is an elusive one, to be honest. I have no idea where to find him. You should try talk to his cousin Charlie near the Blue Moon Inn in Varrock. Maybe he will know.

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Need help finding anyone else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find Checkal?

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Last I heard Checkal was hanging around Barbarian Village. Maybe you should head there to find him?

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Need help finding anyone else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find Burntof?

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Burntof likes to hang out at the Rising Sun Inn located in Falador.

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Need help finding anyone else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I find the entrance to the ruins?

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: On the western side of Ice Mountain. There's no way up on this side so you'll need to head east of here first and go around the dwarf colony to get there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing, thanks.

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Hurry up! It's not exactly exciting standing out here all day.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I seem to have forgotten where Atlas is. Can you remind me?

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: Atlas can be found in the hall to the north of us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: No problem at all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. I'm [player name] and I'm looking for a man named Checkal.

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: That would be me [sir/miss]. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was told to come find you. Willow has found a new digsi...

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: Stop right there [sir/miss]. You can tell Willow she can do the heavy lifting herself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And why is that?

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: Willow does not appreciate the amount of work that goes into building my physique. I would not help her lift a shrimp.

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: I take great pride in these muscles and it hurts me to the core to hear such insults!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But we need your help! She's found a new archaeological digsite and needs you to help clear the entrance.

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: Archaeological digsite? Why is Willow interested in starting a digsite?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She said there could be some ancient ruins, even an untouched hoard of gold and treasure!

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: Ah that makes sense. *Sighs* You poor [lad/lass]...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: Nothing. Look, I will help you. But first you must prove your strength to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well Checkal, I don't mean to brag, but there are few people in Gielinor stronger than me.

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: Hmm... I will see about that.

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: Yes... you do have tremendous muscle mass.

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: Your legs are strong as steel, too.

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: Well [player name], I have to say you are the definition of strength.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I do try.

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: But a [strongman/strongwoman] doesn't stop when they reach the top. They always push themselves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that shouldn't be too hard. I'm pretty strong.

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: That is true. But are you strong in both body and mind?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uhm... yes?

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: To be a true [strongman/strongwoman], you do not just need big muscles. No, you must have self control and an unrelenting desire to push yourself to become stronger.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I'm not sure about that...

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: I would ask you to train with my old friend and trainer, Atlas, one of the strongest barbarians I know. You can find him in the hall north of here.

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: If you can survive his rigorous training program, I shall offer you my help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you know he'll train me?

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: Tell him I sent you and he will train you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright then, I'll go speak to Atlas. Farewell.

lines.speaker: Atlas
lines.text: What do you want, little [man/woman]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've just come to talk to you. Checkal said you could train me. He said you're the strongest barbarian he knows.

lines.speaker: Atlas
lines.text: Oh did he now? What makes you think I will train you?

lines.speaker: Atlas
lines.text: Come on then! Out with it already!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well uh, he said he wouldn't...

lines.speaker: Atlas
lines.text: I'm just pulling your leg! Any friend of Checkal is a friend of mine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please don't scare me like that.

lines.speaker: Atlas
lines.text: It's a force of habit. It comes with the barbarian lifestyle. But to apologise, I'll give you a free lesson!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh really? How much would this usually cost?

lines.speaker: Atlas
lines.text: Around 25,000 coins per session.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's a lot of money!

lines.speaker: Atlas
lines.text: No price is too much for a good workout. Are you ready to start your training session?

lines.speaker: Atlas
lines.text: If you want to become a [strongman/strongwoman], you need to get into shape!

lines.speaker: Atlas
lines.text: It's not all about strength! You need to get some cardio in, too.

lines.speaker: Atlas
lines.text: You need to be tough to be a [strongman/strongwoman]! Show me what you've got! Come on!

lines.speaker: Atlas
lines.text: Every [strongman/strongwoman] was once a beginner who refused to give up.

lines.speaker: Atlas
lines.text: You need to push yourself!

lines.speaker: Atlas
lines.text: Always strive to be the best that you can be! That is the [strongman/strongwoman] way.

lines.speaker: Atlas
lines.text: I'm very proud of you. You've shown grit, determination and an iron will. You are a true [strongman/strongwoman] at heart.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That was exhausting... I've never worked so hard in my life!

lines.speaker: Atlas
lines.text: And for that, I am proud. Now, show us what you're made of and flex those muscles!

lines.speaker: Atlas
lines.text: My work is done. Return to Checkal and show him your new-found muscles.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not right now, I am a bit busy.

lines.speaker: Atlas
lines.text: No problem. Come back when you are ready.

sections.title: If the player said no to training session

lines.speaker: Atlas
lines.text: You're back. Would you like to start that training session now?

lines.speaker: Atlas
lines.text: Well done, little one. You've proven your worth.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I do it again?

lines.speaker: Atlas
lines.text: What? Why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was fun, I'd like to do it again.

lines.speaker: Atlas
lines.text: You're the first person to ask me that [player name]. I suppose I could train you again, but I will have to charge you the full 25,000 coins this time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's do this, I'm ready.

lines.speaker: Atlas
lines.text: It's not going to be any easier my friend!

lines.speaker: Atlas
lines.text: Hold it there little one, you don't have enough money to pay for the lesson.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, sorry I was too excited...

lines.speaker: Atlas
lines.text: No worries friend, come back when you can afford the session.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for that workout. I can see why you normally charge people so much.

lines.speaker: Atlas
lines.text: Back again I see, here for another workout?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Atlas
lines.text: You must take your training very seriously [player name]. This is the second time you'll have done this workout and paid full price!

lines.speaker: Atlas
lines.text: Back again I see, here for another workout?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Atlas
lines.text: You take your training very seriously [player name]. You know you've done this workout [times done] times, are you sure you want to do it again?

sections.title: Talking to Checkal after completing Atlas' training

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: So have you completed your training session with Atlas?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Just about, but it was a real struggle.

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: It always is, but I can see you are standing taller and looking stronger already.

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: Now, show me your muscles and I will judge if you're ready to receive my help.

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: My goodness, those muscles are exquisite. I will definitely help a [man/lady] who shows that much dedication to their physique.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fantastic! Willow said she'd meet you by the entrance, on the west side of Ice Mountain.

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: Thank you my friend, I will meet you there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What an exhausting workout. I hope the others aren't as much effort to recruit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that's everyone. I guess I should head off the entrance on the west side of Ice Mountain.

sections.title: Talking to Checkal again before showing muscles
Talking to Checkal at Ice Mountain

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think you'll be able to handle all these rocks?

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: Should not be a problem. This challenge is easy for a [strongman/strongwoman].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wish I could help...

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: Do not worry my friend. If you stay dedicated and keep working everyday, you'll be able to achieve anything you dream of.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks Checkal, I will.

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: One last thing - be careful when we go inside. You never know what will happen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you want me to do again?

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: I want you to prove your strength by showing me your muscles!

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Buy ush a drink.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you Burntof?

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: I might be, I might not. Buy ush a drink and find out *hic*.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine... I'll get you a beer.

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Thanksh a bunch.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It just so happens I've got a drink you can have.

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Giv' it 'ere then.

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: That'sh good shtuff.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, are you Burntof?

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Yesh I am *hic*. What can thish dwarf do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Willow sent me. She needs your help with a job.

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Willow? Now that'sh a name I drink to forget!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Willow alwaysh insishted on changing me waysh. I knows explosivesh. Just because I lost a couple of me fingersh a few yearsh back doesn't mean I'll do it again *hic*.

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: I knew the exhact amount of dyn*hic*amite needed to blow that rich fool's shafe. We woulda been shet for life!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought you were a team of archaeologists?

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Archaeologishts? Shure, if that's what you want to call ush?!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Willow sent me to get you. She's found a new digsite and needs your expert knowledge on explosives.

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Hmmm... *hic*

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Tell you what. If you beat me in a friendly game of rock paper s*hic*cissorsh, I'll join the team.

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: *Whispers* We'll have the game upshtairs away from everyone.

sections.title: Talking to Burntof again before he gets a beer

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Where'sh my beer?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get right on it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Ah! A [lad/lass] of fhine taste *hic*. I love Dwarven shtout!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, are you Burntof?

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: That'sh good shtuff.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, are you Burntof?

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: No messhing around with you! Only the strong stuff, I like it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, are you Burntof?

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: The rulesh are ash followsh. We both pick a shign to show and then shay 'rock, paper, shcissors.' After schissors, we show our shigns.

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Thish ish a besht of three game. Nowsh, pick what you are going to shhhow.

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Looksh like you win the firsht round.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But...? I put out [chosen sign]?

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Yesh, and I put out *hic* [losing sign]... I think.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay then... I guess the first round goes to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I won! That's two rounds to me, so you have to help.

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Hooold it right there *hic* bud, I thinksh I won that round...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But [chosen sign] beats [losing sign]?

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Yesh, that is correct, but you chose [sign that gets beaten by Burntof's]. So I win.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Whatever, let's just play the last round.

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Well, it looksh like I win, sho I don't have to help you out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you okay Burntof? I clearly beat you. Check my hand again.

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Merlinsh beard! You did. A deal'sh a deal, I'll help you out.

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: But I think I'll need one more drink before I hitsh the road.

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Tina! Bring me another beer pleash.

lines.speaker: Tina
lines.text: Sure thing Burntof, I'll be right there!

lines.speaker: Tina
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Thanksh Tina.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That was interesting, but that's Burntof recruited. On to the next person.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that's everyone. I guess I should head off the entrance on the west side of Ice Mountain.

sections.title: Talking to Burntof at Ice Mountain

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You managed to make your way over to the ruins then?

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Yush, I did, why's that shuch a *hic* shurprise?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought you would pass out on your way.

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Dwarshves have a very good shense of direction and we can *hic* hold our beer very well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure you'll be able to handle the dynamite in your current state?

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Hah! I could blow thish rubble away with one hand and me eyesh closed.

lines.speaker: Charlie the Tramp
lines.text: Spare some change guv?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for a man named Marley. Have you seen him?

lines.speaker: Charlie the Tramp
lines.text: Ah Marley, my cousin. He's always getting himself into trouble!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh really? Do you know where I could find him?

lines.speaker: Charlie the Tramp
lines.text: Hmm... it's been a while since I've seen him. Last I heard he got caught in the middle of a robbery and had to flee Varrock.

lines.speaker: Charlie the Tramp
lines.text: Something to do with trying to steal a set of Wizard robes from the museum.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, so I'm dealing with a criminal!

lines.speaker: Charlie the Tramp
lines.text: Afraid so. Marley is no stranger to the authorities... Knowing him, he's probably hanging out in Edgeville ruins till the attention dies down. I'd try there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perfect. I'll go look for him.

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: Have you got the sandwich yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it. Where did you say you got them from?

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: The Blue Moon Inn, in Varrock. They have the best cook around!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go speak to them, then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey there. Would you happen to be Marley?

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: I could be. Depends on who's asking? If you're with the Varrock City Guard, I had nothing to do with the robbery at the museum. I don't even know what I'd do with some Wizard robes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uhm, no? What robbery? Wizard robes?

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: Robbery? Sorry [sir/miss], first I've heard of a robbery.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look, I'm not here with the city guard, Willow sent me to find you. She needs your help.

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: Willow? Hmm... I'm afraid I don't recall a Willow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure? She said you were old pals.

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: Nope, I'm sure. Only Willow I know of is a type of tree.

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: But, then again, my memory is a bit hazy, due to how hungry I am.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're too hungry... to remember your friend?

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: Yep... been a while since I had a nice hot meal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: *Sighs*... would you like me to get you something to eat, to help jog your memory?

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: Oh what a kind offer! I couldn't say no.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine, I'll be back in a...

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: Woah woah woah, hold your horses there friend! I can't just eat anything. I have very specific dietary requirements.

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: Don't be like that! I don't want nothing fancy, just a Steak sandwich from the Blue Moon Inn, in Varrock.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why don't you go and get one then?

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: Slight problem there, friend. I've been barred and the chef won't make me another one. Something about stealing from punters. I can't remember...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: *Sighs*... Fine. If I go get you this sandwich, will you help Willow?

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: Of course! Oh, by the way, don't bother with the bartender. Just talk to the chef. Trust me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright. I'll be back momentarily with your sandwich.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: What do you want? I'm busy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was wondering if you'd be able to make me a Steak sandwich?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Hmm... we don't get many food orders in 'ere. Last person to order one of them was barred for thievin'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... uhm... no. It's for me?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No... it's for Marley...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: I knew it! I'm not making another meal for that thievin' git! Clear off.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: At least tell me why you hate him so much?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: I know what it's like, not knowin' where your next meals going to come from, so I let him eat 'ere for free.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: The minute I turn my back, he's caught red-handed trying to pickpocket one of our patrons. We gave him the boot there 'n' then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, fair enough, but it's just one sandwich. He's not even in Varrock anymore. He's moved off to Edgeville.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: No chance mate, it's the principle. I won't make food for such a low class criminal...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please, I really need Marley's help and he'll only offer it if I get him a Steak sandwich.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Well, I suppose if I give you the recipe, you can make it yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! I can't imagine it's that hard to make.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Watch your mouth! Making a Steak sandwich is a delicate art!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry! Of course it is. What's the recipe then?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: To start, you need to get some prime beef from a cow. The fields around Lumbridge are a great source. Cook it up - medium rare is perfect. Don't cook it for too long or you'll burn it...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Then, you take some bread. I recommend making it fresh, but to each their own. Slice it in half and slide the beautifully cooked beef between the two halves...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: And hey presto, you've got yourself a delicious, perfectly made Steak sandwich.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow... is that it? Some beef between a loaf of bread?

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: It's harder to make than it sounds! Now get out before I whack you one for being so rude.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: What do you want? I'm busy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I forgot the recipe to make a Steak sandwich.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: For Saradomin's sake! Take some bread, cut it in half and stick some cooked Steak inside, done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yeah, thank you!

sections.title: Talking to the cook after managing to make yourself a Steak sandwich

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: What do you want? I'm busy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've managed to make myself a Steak sandwich.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Let me take a look at it.

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: Ha ha ha, you call this a sandwich!?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I tried my best...

lines.speaker: Cook
lines.text: I'm sure you did. It'll be enough for Marley, anyway.

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: What can I do yer for?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, Marley sent me in here for a Steak sandwich.

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: MARLEY?! Don't talk about that sorry state of a person in this establishment!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Woah okay, sorry! Forget I ever mentioned him.

lines.speaker: Bartender
lines.text: You'd better clear off before I make you.

sections.title: Talking to Marley at Ice Mountain

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again Marley.

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: Ah, my new favourite chef. Have you got another sandwich for me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: Woah! Alright there, calm down. Was only a joke.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't find being your errand boy very funny.

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: You need to lighten up a little, my friend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey Marley. I've managed to get your Steak sandwich.

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: Ah, just in time for my afternoon snack. Pass it over.

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: Aaah... not quite as good as the usual, but it hit the spot.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you finally done?

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: Yes [sir/miss] I am. I'll honour my side of the deal and head off to find Willow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Finally! You can find her on the west side of Ice Mountain.

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: Excellent! I'll be on my way then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Marley is certainly an interesting character. Hopefully the next team member is more respectable.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that's everyone. I guess I should head off the entrance on the west side of Ice Mountain.

sections.title: Talking to Willow again if the player chose no

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Are you finally ready to crack these ruins open?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's everyone gathered. Interesting company for an archaeologist to keep...

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: *Shrugs* How many archaeologists do you actually know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I-

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Exactly. So who are you to judge? Anyway, we're all here and ready to break in... Um, I mean excavate... the entrance.

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Are you ready? Who knows what's inside the ruins?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm ready as I'll ever be. Let's head in.

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Right, that's everyone gathered. Thank you, [player name], for all your help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh it was no pro...

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Okay everyone, we've wasted enough time. Let's crack this door open. Burntof, you're up!

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Try not to blow the entire mountain up this time...

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Yesh ma'am, let'sh get this rock out the way.

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Jusht got to plashe thish here...

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: ... little bit of dyn-*hic*-amite here...

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: ... and there we go!

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Now we light the fushe!

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: What are you doin' standin' there?!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Everyone'sh ran for cover [lad/lass]. You'd best do the shame.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait, what?!

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Clock'sh ticking [lad/lass]!

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: Bloomin' 'eck, Burntof!

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: That was a tad too explosive I think.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: My ears are still ringing!

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Quit yer moanin', it did the job didnae it?

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: He's right, the rock's been broken up. Checkal, it's your turn.

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: Of course ma'am, I will get to work.

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: The work is done, Willow. My apologies if it took too long.

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Don't be sorry, Checkal. Gotta put those muscles to use somehow.

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Alright Marley, in you go. Let us know if you find anything.

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: Right, in I go. Wish me luck [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Try not to die...

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: Right, let's see what hidden surprises you have in store for me.

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: Hmm...

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: That would have been nasty.

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: Is that it? I thought this place would be better protected.

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: Now to disarm it...

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: Maybe this will work?

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: That got it!

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: Alright guys! It's safe to come in! I think...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, this place is amazing. I can't believe this was hidden away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wonder how long it's been left untouched for.

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: You're impressed? There's nothing here but dust and fallen rocks.

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Yaaa*hic*aawn

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't find it exciting? As an archaeologist, I thought you would be thrilled at this discovery?

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Thrilled? What's there to be excited about? I don't see any gold, diamonds, rare gems or piles of lost treasure!

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Hmm...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not about the money though, right? We've potentially discovered the long lost ruins of an ancient civilisation!

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Of course it's about the money. It's always been about money, you fool.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't understand...

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: We're not a team of archaeologist, you idiot. We're grave robbers looking for their next score.

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Theshe markings look familiar...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So this entire time, you've just been using me to help break into these ruins in hopes of finding someone else's treasures.

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Yep, and you were surprisingly easy to convince. I'll admit, you have been a tremendous help, [player name].

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: But your usefulness has come to an end I'm afraid. I can't simply let you leave here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: I can't risk you running to the authorities and telling them about our little gang. We're wanted in quite a few large cities across Gielinor.

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: I've sheen thish before...

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: I'm afraid this is the end of the road for you, [player name]. This ruin is about to become your tomb.

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: *hic* Woops...

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: What was that noise?

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Uh... it wasn't me!

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Whats happening?!

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: It feels like the quaking has finally stopped...

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Holy Zamorak! What is that thing?! Checkal, take it down!

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: I'm a muscle man, not a fighter.

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: I think we should get out of here, Willow!

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: You're right, Marley. Burntof, we're leaving!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't believe they ran so quickly. I suppose I should take care of this thing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need a little bit more time to prepare.

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Hurry up then! We've waited long enough.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That monster showed up at the perfect time. I would have been toast if Willow and her gang had their chance to attack.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Speaking of which, maybe they left something behind I can salvage some kind of reward from.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Looks like Willow left a bag behind. Maybe there's something in it I could take.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Those will do nicely!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was that noise? Please don't be another rock monster...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What the!

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Bless my beard...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow. I honestly didn't expect to see a dwarf in these ruins.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: I can't believe it...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, exactly.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: It's been so long since I've heard another person's voice.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: How did you find me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: To be honest, it was all a bit of an accident.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There was a gang of... well, I thought were archaeologists, but it turned out they were grave robbers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I helped them clear the entrance to these ruins and we worked our way inside, only to wake up a monster! They ran off and left me to defeat it.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: That wasn't a monster...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well it looked menacing enough!

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: That was one of the guardians of this once great city.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: City?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Yes! Camdozaal, home of the Imcando Dwarves.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: These halls once bustled with life, but that was a long time ago. I'm the only one left now. The name's Ramarno.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well Ramarno, I'm [player name], and you have me at a loss for words! I'd have never even imagined there would be a whole city down here.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Not much of a city anymore, I suppose.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess I didn't help with that. Sorry about breaking into your home and killing your guardian.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: We have plenty more. No real harm done. That said, I do think I might have a way for you to make up for it. There'll likely be something in it for you as well.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: My workshop is just north of here. Meet me there and we'll talk further.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So this is where you scampered off to after the monster awoke?

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Look [player name], it was a simple misunderstanding, that's all...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh really? You threatening to kill me was just a misunderstanding?

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Things got a little heated down there, but we all made it out alive, right?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Barely... I had to defeat that monster on my own!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing out here, anyway?

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: We thought you'd rat us out to the authorities, so we came out here to hide.

lines.speaker: Willow
lines.text: Plus, we heard there are some sealed tombs in the desert that haven't been touched in hundreds of years...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, of course... scoping out your next grave to rob.

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: [player name], I am so glad to see you are safe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Checkal. Why didn't you tell me about Willow?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought we were friends...

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: I am sorry [player name]. I do respect you and your love for the muscle. But sadly I needed the money. These muscles don't pay the bills on their own.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I understand...

lines.speaker: Checkal
lines.text: Please do not let this incident divert you from your path of becoming a [strongman/strongwoman]! I truly believe in you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How you holding up Burntof? Must have had quite the fright when you woke that monster.

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: You're right [lad/lass]. It did give me a little fright.

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: But I saw some interesting things down in the ruins...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: Some of the markings on the wall look very familiar to me. I've seen them before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, when I defeated the monster, an Imcando dwarf opened the barred door...

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: An Imcando dwarf! I thought they were all but extinct! You've found something truly amazing [player name]!

lines.speaker: Burntof
lines.text: I wish I had a drink to celebrate, but this desert is bone dry.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, the desert really suits you Marley.

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: [player name] my old pal! How are you doing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Better now I don't have to deal with you. Enjoying life in the desert?

lines.speaker: Marley
lines.text: Not really. I'm not suited to such harsh climates. No steak sandwiches here either...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh what a shame! Poor Marley's going to starve as he doesn't have anyone to make his sandwiches... So sad.
